name: Sonic the Hedgehog
description:
Sonic the Hedgehog is a side-scrolling platformer centered around speed and momentum. The player controls Sonic, a blue hedgehog with the ability to run at incredible speeds. The core gameplay loop involves navigating linear levels filled with obstacles, enemies (primarily robots called Badniks), and collectible rings. The objective is to reach the end of each level while collecting as many rings as possible. Rings act as both a score and a form of health; upon being hit by an enemy, Sonic loses all held rings, and if hit without any rings, he loses a life. 

Levels are designed with branching paths, loops, and slopes that encourage maintaining speed. Sonic can jump to overcome obstacles, defeat enemies, and access higher areas. He can also perform a 'spin dash,' a move where he curls into a ball and charges up energy to launch himself forward at high speed. Hidden within levels are special stages accessible by finding giant rings. These bonus stages offer opportunities to collect Chaos Emeralds, which are essential for unlocking the game's true ending.

Enemies generally patrol fixed areas or follow simple patterns. Sonic can defeat them by jumping on them in a curled-up position or using the spin dash. Obstacles include pitfalls, spikes, moving platforms, and destructible barriers. The game features distinct zones, each with its unique aesthetic theme and environmental hazards. Each zone culminates in a boss battle against Dr. Robotnik (also known as Eggman) and his mechanical creations.

controls.arrow_down: Initiate Spin Dash when stationary; Duck when moving (prepares for spin dash); allows Sonic to roll down slopes more easily.
controls.shift: Hold to maintain maximum running speed (speed boost)
controls.w: Look up (limited function, mostly for viewing the level)
controls.s: Look down (limited function, mostly for viewing the level)
